About Us

Vision Intelligence builds reliable, holistic solutions that make industries like construction safer and more secure. We design and manufacture our own hardware — solar-powered security cameras, environmental monitoring stations, and traffic monitoring systems — write the software that powers it, and deliver hands-on installation and support from more than twenty branches across Australia and New Zealand.

We've grown steadily for over a decade and are expanding our core customer experience platform to serve more industries and more customers. Our fleet is set to grow 20% over the next year.

About the Role

We're looking for a Tech Lead to join our product organisation and lead a team of engineers building our next-generation Site Awareness platform — the core customer experience used by 80% of our customers.

This is a hands-on, high-impact engineering leadership role. You'll start by helping establish platforms and practices for a new team, then lead your own team of engineers delivering bespoke solutions for high-value customers and incubating new products.

What you'll do

• Lead and mentor a team of engineers building our next-generation customer experience platform.
• Translate business objectives into clear technical designs, delivery plans, and actionable work.
• Ship high-quality, maintainable software while ensuring reliability and scalability.
• Define how software is shipped — from local development through to deployment, updates, and observability.
• Establish patterns for reliability, observability, and maintainability across the platform.
• Collaborate with the CEO, product leaders, sales reps, and customers to understand problems and turn them into software that matters.
• Sequence complex work in a way that supports quality and efficiency.
• Encourage smart experimentation while keeping a strong focus on results and customer impact.
• Spot patterns that can be extracted and reused, and advocate to simplify or decommission solutions that are too variable to operate at scale.

Minimum 5+ years of team leadership experience in engineering roles is required.

About You

You're a smart creative — a builder, a leader, and an innovator. You combine strong technical skills with product and business sense. You can zoom from high-level strategy to hands-on code and you're motivated by delivering real value to customers.

You bring:

• Deep technical craft: Expert in TypeScript with strong Python and DevOps capability (infrastructure as code, CI/CD pipelines, observability). Deep experience with AWS services.
• Business and customer focus: You think about the "why," not just the "how." You understand how code choices affect user experience and business value.
• Creative confidence: You're curious, experimental, and unafraid to challenge assumptions. You prototype and iterate instead of waiting for perfect conditions.
• Leadership in ambiguity: Comfortable steering a team in uncertain territory — framing objectives, sequencing complex work, and ensuring outcomes are high-quality and impactful.
• Collaborative communication: You thrive on debate, feedback, and shared ownership. You talk as comfortably with engineers as with sales or customers.
• Lean delivery: Experienced with Kanban-based delivery and optimising flow from daily to quarterly resolutions.

Minimum 5+ years of hands-on TypeScript development experience is essential.

Minimum 3-5 years of practical AWS services experience is required.

You know how to:

• Write software that runs on servers, in browsers, and on resource-constrained devices.
• Manage cloud infrastructure declaratively.
• Define continuous integration and deployment pipelines.
• Manage and version data and schemas.
• Design security into a system.
• Apply lean software development principles.

Our Hiring Process

We've designed our process to showcase how you think, create, and collaborate:

Send us a short note (~20 mins) — Tell us about something you've built that best demonstrates your skills.

Chat with us (~40 mins) — We'll ask about your past work and how you'd approach challenges here.

Build with us (~3 hrs) — Work on an example problem together so we can see how we'd collaborate in real life.

Why Vision Intelligence?

• End-to-end ownership: hardware, software, and customer experience.
• Steady, profitable growth — not hype-driven.
• Real impact: our technology makes construction sites safer and more secure every day.
• A team that values simplicity, accountability, transparency, and continuous learning.

About IMDEX
At IMDEX, we create technology that efficiently and sustainably unlocks the Earth’s value. Our technologies and products allow smarter and safer decisions for people, assets and the earth. Through innovation and curiosity, we continue to find new ways to improve accuracy and efficiency in mining in a more sustainable way. For further information visit www.imdex.com.




The Opportunity

As part of IMDEX’s continued investment in digital solutions, we’re seeking an experienced Product Manager to join our Digital Earth Knowledge (DEK) team. This is a global role with end‑to‑end ownership of a defined digital product, covering strategy, development, commercialisation, and lifecycle management.

Your initial focus will be on ioGAS, a market‑leading desktop application used by geoscientists worldwide to analyse, interpret, and visualise complex geoscience data, particularly in geochemistry and subsurface interpretation. You will play a pivotal role in shaping the direction, roadmap, and success of the established and evolving digital product with a global customer base.

You’ll work closely with Engineering, Sales, Marketing, and customers to ensure the product delivers real value — balancing technical depth, user needs, and commercial outcomes.




Key Responsibilities

In this role, you will:

Own the end‑to‑end product lifecycle for a digital geoscience product, from opportunity assessment through to growth and retirement

Define and deliver product strategy, roadmaps, and delivery priorities aligned with the broader IMDEX portfolio

Lead customer discovery and validation activities to inform product decisions

Translate user needs into clear requirements, user stories, and release plans

Partner closely with Engineering to guide development and ensure customer outcomes are achieved

Collaborate with Marketing on product positioning, pricing, and go‑to‑market strategy

Act as a product evangelist, supporting customers, sales teams, and industry engagement

Monitor market trends, competitor offerings, and emerging technologies (including AI/ML)

Support successful global product launches and ongoing adoption




What You’ll Bring

You’re a confident and curious Product Manager with a strong ability to bridge the gap between technical depth and user value. You enjoy working with complex data products, engaging deeply with customers, and influencing cross‑functional teams to deliver meaningful outcomes.




Qualifications & Experience:

Essential:

3+ years’ experience as a Product Manager for a data‑driven, analytics, GIS, or scientific software product

Proven experience owning product roadmaps, prioritisation, and delivery

Strong ability to translate complex user needs into clear, actionable requirements

Solid understanding of data analysis and visualisation concepts

Experience working with both technical and non‑technical stakeholders

Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain trade‑offs and decisions clearly

Desirable:

Domain knowledge in geoscience, geochemistry, mining, or subsurface data

Experience with GIS, spatial analysis, or geospatial visualisation

Exposure to enterprise or B2B software products

Familiarity with ioGAS or similar geoscience software

Ability to define adoption metrics and success measures beyond feature delivery

Basic Python or R skills for analysis or prototyping




At IMDEX - we do things differently

IMDEX is a unique blend of technology and mining. Our people have contrasting and complementary skills which drive innovation, adaptability, and holistic problem solving.

We offer our global talent:

A culture of care – we care and look out for our people by fostering an inclusive and respectful working environment.

Game changing work – we give you autonomy and provide flexibility that harnesses your strengths and individuality.

Opportunities to grow – we want you to grow and expand your thinking and provide clear career pathways so you can see where you can go next.

Empowerment by purpose – to be close to the solution and change the face of mining forever.

Ability to build strong connections within a global business – you will have the opportunity to collaborate with people from all over the world.




IMDEX is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to employing a diverse workforce and providing a workplace of respect and inclusiveness. We place great emphasis on ensuring our company is a safe, high performing and welcoming workplace.

The Organisation

Airservices Australia is a government-owned organisation responsible for the safe and efficient management of 11% of the world’s airspace and provision of aviation rescue fire fighting services at Australia’s busiest airports.

Our people are our greatest asset with a dynamic and diverse team operating from locations across the country - from bustling cities to regional and remote locations, including an island. This team keep are helping to secure the future of Australia’s skies and keep it amongst the safest in the world.

We connect people with their world safely – linking family and friends, generating economic activity, creating jobs, and facilitating trade and tourism.

Airservices is committed to fostering a culture that is diverse, inclusive, and respectful.  We encourage motivated individuals who love what they do, value a service first mindset and embrace a challenge to explore a career with Airservices. In return you will be a valued team member, be offered flexibility and experience a meaningful career in an exciting, ever-evolving aviation industry.

The Role & The Team

We are seeking an experienced Senior Portfolio Analyst to play a key role in a fixed term for 6 months in driving financial and performance excellence across Airservices’ capital investment portfolio.

Working closely with the Investment Manager Lead, portfolio leaders and project managers, you will provide high-quality financial analysis, reporting and insight that supports effective decision-making across our capital allocation and investment pipeline. As a senior member of the Investment Management team, you will help ensure our Investment Plan remains current, transparent and aligned to organisational priorities and governance requirements.

This is a highly collaborative role that partners across projects, programs and portfolios, influencing outcomes through strong commercial acumen, trusted relationships and clear financial insight.

In this role, you will:

Provide strategic financial and commercial advice to senior leaders, executives and key stakeholders across the organisation
Maintain and manage the Airservices 10-year Investment Plan (ASIP), including funded investments and unfunded proposals
Lead month-end portfolio activities including forecasting, accruals, variance analysis and financial commentary
Deliver insightful portfolio-level reporting and analysis to support investment prioritisation and decision-making
Develop and maintain financial models, tools and dashboards to improve transparency, governance and compliance
Drive the standardisation and simplification of financial processes, frameworks and ways of working
Build financial capability across the project and portfolio community through coaching, mentoring and capability uplift
Foster strong, collaborative relationships across finance, transformation, portfolio management and senior leadership teams

You are a commercially astute finance professional who thrives in complex, matrixed environments and enjoys partnering with stakeholders to influence outcomes.

Strong financial acumen with exposure to capital investment prioritisation and portfolio management

Lead Western’s institutional strategy and embed it through planning and performance.

The Opportunity
As the Senior Lead, Strategy and Planning, you will shape, steward and embed the University’s institutional strategy and long-term strategic direction. You will provide authoritative strategic leadership that helps translate ambition into clear priorities, choices and success measures across the University.

Operating as a trusted advisor within the Office of the Vice-Chancellor, you will support the Vice-Chancellor and University Executive with strategic framing, options and advice in a complex and evolving higher education environment. Reporting to the Chief of Staff, you will bring influence, judgement and clarity to executive decision-making and governance discussions.

You will lead horizon scanning, scenario planning and strategic intelligence to inform long-term decisions, and you will design and coordinate an integrated planning framework that aligns institutional priorities, divisional plans and enabling strategies. You will also oversee strategic performance measures and insights, delivering executive-level papers, options analyses and reporting that strengthen accountability and focus on impact.

What Success Looks Like:

A clear, future-focused institutional strategy that is well understood and translated into priorities and success measures.
High-quality horizon scanning and scenario planning that strengthens executive decision-making and positioning.
An integrated planning framework that aligns institutional priorities with divisional plans and governance cycles.
Executive-ready strategy papers, options analyses and performance insights that enable timely, evidence-based decisions.
Performance measures and reporting that support accountability, identify risks and opportunities, and drive continuous improvement.


Please refer to the Position Description for full details.

About You

Tertiary qualifications in a relevant discipline, or equivalent extensive experience.
Demonstrated success leading enterprise-level strategy in a large, complex organisation.
Strong conceptual, analytical and systems-thinking capability, with the ability to frame complex issues and options.
Highly developed written and verbal communication skills, including preparation of executive and governance materials.
Proven ability to lead with authority, influence without formal power, and set and monitor performance metrics.


Benefits
We invest in the people who make our mission possible. Alongside a rewarding career, we offer a range of benefits designed to support your financial, personal and family wellbeing.

Financial benefits: Access to staff-only products and offers with selected banking partners, salary sacrifice options for onsite parking, and novated motor vehicle leasing.
Health and wellbeing support: Free Employee Assistance Program (EAP), complimentary annual flu vaccination, eligibility for Fitness Passport membership, and access to discounted private health cover.
Onsite childcare: Long day care and early learning services operate at our Campbelltown, Hawkesbury, Parramatta, and Penrith campuses. Staff may be eligible for discounted fees and salary packaging, with places subject to centre capacity.


Application Details

Position Enquiries: Direct to Sharmaine Gewohn, Chief of Staff via S.Gewohn@westernsydney.edu.au. Please include the job reference code REF1224W. 
Closing Date: 11:59pm, Sunday 26 April 2026.
Working Rights: You must provide evidence of full-time working rights in Australia at application.
How to Apply: Please note that only applications submitted via the University’s online recruitment system will be considered. As part of your online application, you will be asked to respond to screening questions addressing the key qualifications, experiences and skills required for this position.


Belonging at Western
We are committed to substantive equality as outlined in our Equal Opportunity Policy. We actively encourage applications from Indigenous Australians, candidates of all gender identities and expressions, people of all abilities, and those from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds.

This is your opportunity to be part of something bigger. By joining Western Sydney University, you become a valued member of our community. As our region changes, so too does our university. Western is boldly different, energetic, global, diverse, dynamic, and youthful.

Help us deliver a better tomorrow

Australia Post is delivering for all Australians. From the vehicles that we drive, to the small businesses that make our communities thrive. We’re delivering for the environment, for our communities, for our customers and for our people. We’re moving forward and we want you to come along for the ride.


About the opportunity
This is a role for someone who can turn customer and performance data into clear business action.


As Experience Performance Manager, you’ll help us understand where the Freight customer experience is working well, where it is breaking down, and what we should do next. You’ll bring together customer feedback, performance data and operational insights to help leaders make better decisions.




You’ll work across Freight Experience, product, pricing, sales, marketing, customer research and operations. Your job is to make the data useful, the story clear and the priorities easier to act on.
What you’ll deliver with us


You’ll deliver the insight that helps improve the end-to-end Freight customer experience.




Success in this role means leaders have a clear view of customer pain points, performance trends and improvement priorities. You’ll build the dashboards, measures and analysis that help the business decide where to focus, what to fix and how to track progress. The role reports to the Group Manager, Performance & Insight.


You’ll also

Build and manage dashboards and performance measures that show how the Freight customer experience is performing
Analyse customer feedback, operational data and performance trends to identify issues, root causes and opportunities
Turn data into clear recommendations that help teams improve service, customer outcomes and business performance


About you
You’re commercially minded, curious and good at making sense of messy or complex information.
You know how to turn data into a clear story. You can spot what matters, explain it simply and help others decide what to do next. You’re comfortable working with senior stakeholders, asking good questions and bringing people with you.
You’re practical, outcome-focused and comfortable working across different teams and priorities. You care about improving the customer experience, not just reporting on it.


You’ll also need




Strong experience in data and performance analysis, customer experience, business intelligence or a similar field
Experience building dashboards and reports using tools like Tableau, Power BI, Looker or similar
Experience building data queries in cloud based data environments.
Strong communication and stakeholder skills, with the ability to present insights clearly to non-technical audiences and senior leaders


How we’ll deliver for you

A role with real influence, where your work helps shape decisions on customer experience, priorities and investment
Broad exposure across Freight Experience, product, pricing, sales, marketing and operations
The chance to improve how customer performance is measured and managed, and help drive better outcomes for customers and the business

At Wesfarmers Health we strive to make health, beauty and wellness experiences simpler, more affordable and easier to access for all Australians.

Our portfolio includes well-known names like Priceline, as well as our MediAesthetics brands - Clear Skincare Clinics and SILK Laser Clinics. In the digital space, we're proud to have SISU Health and InstantScripts—and most recently, we've launched our newest retail beauty stores, atomica.





About us:

What began in 1910 as a small co-operative of just three pharmacies has grown into Wesfarmers Health—a leading Australian organisation in health, beauty, and wellness.

Today, Wesfarmers Health proudly employs over 3,600 team members, all committed to delivering accessible, innovative, and trusted health and beauty services to Australians nationwide, across retail, medi-aesthetics, digital health, and wholesale and supply chain operations.

What you'll do:

We are seeking a Pricing Analyst to join our Commercial Excellence team on a 2 years Fixed Term contract. Reporting to the Head of Pricing & Value, you'll support the development, execution and monitoring of pricing and value initiatives across both retail and wholesale channels.

This is an ideal opportunity for an early‑career analyst looking to build strong commercial and pricing capability within a dynamic, fast‑paced retail environment. You'll turn data into insights, helping the business make informed, compliant and value‑driven pricing decisions.

Key activities include:

Analysing pricing changes, cost movements and margin impacts
Assisting with scenario and sensitivity analysis for pricing and promotional decisions
Producing regular pricing, margin and profitability reports across retail, wholesale and online channels
Tracking pricing KPIs and highlighting key risks, variances and opportunities
Monitoring competitor pricing, promotions and market trends
Maintaining competitor benchmarks and supporting price positioning insights
Supporting pricing governance, documentation and audit processes
Partnering with Category, Finance and Wholesale teams on pricing initiatives

If you're analytically minded, commercially curious and enjoy working across data, stakeholders and real‑world business decisions, this role offers a strong platform for growth within a leading retail organisation.

What you'll bring:

A minimum of two years' experience in pricing, commercial analysis, finance, or data analytics (retail or wholesale exposure desirable but not essential)
Strong analytical skills with high attention to detail
Intermediate to advanced Excel skills; experience with BI tools (e.g. Power BI) advantageous
Ability to interpret data and translate findings into clear, practical insights
Strong communication skills and confidence working with cross functional stakeholders
Well organised, proactive, and able to manage multiple priorities in a fast paced environment
Willingness to learn pricing frameworks, governance, and commercial best practice
Bachelor's degree in Commerce, Finance, Economics, Data Analytics, or a related field

Why us?

Access to employee benefits across Wesfarmers including team member discounts at Bunnings, Kmart, Target, Officeworks, OnePass, Priceline, atomica and Clear Skincare Clinics
Ongoing professional development and career opportunities across the Wesfarmers Health Division and the broader Wesfarmers Group
Novated leasing options
Access to our Employee Assistance Program (EAP) - available to all team members and their immediate family members, 24/7, 365 days a year
Beautiful outdoor terrace for work and recreation
Food, coffee and health & wellbeing facilities onsite including a landlord operated gym
Dedicated end of trip facilities (cycle racks, showers, lockers)

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Applicants

Wesfarmers Health aims to become an employer of choice for First Nations Australians, through investments that attract, empower and retain First Nations team members within our stores, distribution centres, clinics and corporate offices.

As part of the Wesfarmers group, we provide authentic support through a diverse range of programs and initiatives, designed to empower you on your journey towards personal achievement and professional advancement.

Design enterprise data pipelines, cloud integrations and analytics platforms to deliver trusted reporting.

Your new company


You'll join a nationally operating government organisation focused on building modern data capability to support operational delivery, reporting, and decision-making. The environment values strong engineering practice, collaboration, and continuous improvement.





Your new role

This role requires a Senior Data Engineer to design, build and maintain enterprise‑grade data solutions that make data accessible, trusted, and usable for reporting, analytics, and operational needs.
The position is responsible for delivering integrated data pipelines across on‑premises and cloud environments, developing consolidated data models to support enterprise‑wide reporting, and contributing to the delivery of modern data warehouse and analytics platforms.
The role also plays a key part in supporting cloud migration and operational uplift, including transitioning data and business‑as‑usual processes, improving workflows, and producing documentation to support sustainable operations. Acting as a technical authority, the position contributes to data engineering standards, governance, and best practice across complex programs of work.


Design and deliver data pipelines that integrate data across on‑premises and cloud platforms to improve reporting, consistency and accessibility.
Develop and maintain trusted, consolidated data models to support enterprise reporting and analytics, with appropriate governance and change controls.
Act as a data engineering subject-matter expert supporting system integration and reporting platform delivery in collaboration with internal and external partners.
Lead and support the migration of data pipelines and operational processes to cloud environments, uplifting workflows and supporting handover to BAU teams.
Provide technical leadership across data engineering practices, ensuring solutions align with agreed architectures, standards and security requirements.

What you'll need to succeed

Essential Criteria

Data engineering: Level 6 (SFIA)
Leads the selection and development of data engineering methods, tools and techniques. Develops organisational policies, standards, and guidelines for the development and secure operation of data services and products. Ensures adherence to technical strategies and architectures. Plans and leads data engineering activities for strategic, large and complex programmes.

Data integrity and quality assurance: Level 3 (DCF)
It is an expert resource in the use of measures for data quality assurance, the interaction of those measures, and their application in conjunction with one another Can advise others on the use of data quality measures to make accurate assessments of data fitness - for purpose (including trustworthiness and accuracy). Is an expert resource on ensuring data integrity across its lifecycle, and advises others.

Data modelling and design: Level 5 (SFIA) Sets standards for data modelling and design tools and techniques, advises on their application and ensures compliance. Manages the investigation of enterprise data requirements based upon a detailed understanding of information requirements. Coordinates the application of analysis, design and modelling techniques to establish, modify or maintain data structures and their associated components. Manages the iteration, review and maintenance of data requirements and data model

Integrate data: Level 3 (DCF)
Can perform and provide expert advice on data integration. Can build the capability of others in understanding and applying good data integration practice and principles. Can write custom scripts and code to integrate data. Maintains understanding of new trends and innovations relating to integrating data (including AI).



What you need to do now

Ad Text:
About the Role 
The Director, Education Innovation Lab is a unique academic leadership role responsible for establishing and leading Swinburne’s Education Innovation Lab as a flagship, university-wide platform for future-focused educational innovation.

Reporting to the Pro Vice-Chancellor, Educational Innovation, the Director will provide strategic leadership to anticipate emerging futures of education and drive the design, testing and validation of new approaches to curriculum, pedagogy, assessment and learner experience. The role will lead a portfolio of programs spanning futures exploration, design-led incubation and rapid prototyping, ensuring that insights and validated models can be translated into scalable institutional practice.

Working in partnership with academic and professional leaders across the University, as well as industry, EdTech and global collaborators, the Director will position the Lab as a catalyst for transformation. The role will also foster a culture of disciplined experimentation, enabling Swinburne to explore bold educational possibilities while maintaining academic quality and integrity.

About the Education Innovation Lab
The Education Innovation Lab is Swinburne’s central platform for educational innovation, focused on systemic transformation across teaching, learning, policy and practice. It brings together educators, students, professional staff and industry partners to reimagine how education is designed, delivered and experienced, responding to complex challenges through collaborative experimentation and rapid prototyping.

About You  
You are an accomplished academic leader with an internationally recognised track record in educational innovation, futures of education, design-led change or related fields. You bring a combination of experience across higher education and industry, enabling you to connect academic thinking with real-world application and shape future-focused education models.

You have demonstrated success leading complex initiatives and bring expertise in futures thinking, emerging technologies and design-led innovation. A collaborative and influential leader, you build strong partnerships, lead high-performing teams and translate innovative ideas into practical, scalable outcomes.

*Please refer to the link at the bottom of this page to view the position description. 

Qualifications

A doctoral qualification in a relevant discipline.
Recognition as a leading authority in education innovation, learning and teaching or higher education transformation.

About Swinburne University of Technology 
Swinburne’s strategy draws upon our understanding of future challenges. We choose to build Swinburne as the prototype of a new and different university – one that is truly of Technology, of Innovation and of Entrepreneurship. We are committed to a differentiated university proposition in education and research.

Our Ad Astra strategy is the cornerstone of Swinburne’s bold ambition to lead globally in technology-driven education and research. This strategy positions us to create transformative solutions, empower learners, and partner with industry to thrive in an ever-changing world.   

What We Offer You!

Work your way with flexible working arrangements, including hybrid options and generous parental leave to support a balanced lifestyle.
Exclusive staff discounts, including discounted Medibank health insurance, 50% off Swinburne courses and other partner offers.
Swinburne offers 17% super and salary packaging options, including car leasing and extra super contributions.

Want to learn more? Head over to our benefits page!

Design and maintain data pipelines while delivering trusted insights from large-scale administrative data.

Your new company

You'll join a large, nationally distributed organisation that places strong value on data quality, analytical excellence, and evidence‑based insights. Technical expertise is recognised, and the environment supports collaboration, rigour, and sustained delivery.



Your new role


This role requires a specialist Data Analyst to support the ongoing development and use of a large-scale national data asset. The position is responsible for designing, building and maintaining high-quality administrative data pipelines and supporting the development of new pipelines used to analyse participant movement across complex education and workforce pathways.
The role is central to delivering core analytical products and maintaining a critical data asset that underpins performance measurement, reporting, and strategic insight generation at a national level.
The position applies advanced data engineering capability and strong analytical expertise to integrate and manage complex administrative datasets, ensuring outputs are accurate, reliable, and delivered on time for government and other stakeholders. Through sustained infrastructure development and rigorous analytics, the role supports the delivery of trusted, evidence‑based insights to inform decision-making.



Key Responsibilities

Design, build and maintain high-quality, robust administrative data pipelines to support complex outcome analysis.
Manage the ingestion, transformation and integration of large administrative datasets from multiple sources, ensuring consistency, accuracy and reliability.
Apply strong subject‑matter and analytical expertise to analyse participant pathways and transitions across interconnected systems.
Implement data quality controls, validation processes and documentation standards to ensure integrity and reproducibility of outputs.
Support the delivery of key analytical products, including performance measures, reports and stakeholder insights.



What you'll need to succeed


Essential Criteria:

Demonstrated advanced coding skills across multiple programming languages.
Expertise in machine learning techniques and experience with large-scale administrative datasets.
Strong knowledge of VET and higher education data and subject matter expertise across both sectors.
Proven experience designing, building and maintaining high quality, robust administrative data pipelines to support analysis across complex policy or service delivery domains.
Desirable criteria

Demonstrated practical experience working within the ABS Person Level Integrated Data Asset (PLIDA), including extracting detailed analytical outputs while adhering to ABS legislative, privacy and statistical disclosure control requirements.
Demonstrated experience working with ATO occupation data in a policy or analytical context.

Job Title: Senior Analyst, Group FP&A - Real Estate Investment
Ad Text:
The Company

Our client is a market-leading ASX-listed property investment group with a high-profile leadership team, strong access to institutional capital and a clearly defined growth strategy. The business is highly acquisitive, continues to launch new funds and manages a large and diversified portfolio spanning premium Commercial, Industrial and Retail assets.

The Role

The Senior Analyst, Group FP&A, sits at the centre of financial planning, analysis and strategic decision-making for the organisation. Working closely with senior leadership and teams across the business, the role supports the delivery of group forecasting, budgeting, performance analysis and capital planning.

Key Responsibilities
Partner with divisional teams to understand key drivers of performance, risks and opportunities, and provide commercial insights.
Maintain and enhance group forecasting models across the income statement, balance sheet and cash flow.
Coordinate and deliver the group budgeting and long-term forecasting process, including consolidation of divisional inputs.
Support capital management through analysis of funding requirements, debt metrics and financial data for rating agencies.
Contribute to monthly and quarterly reporting for senior leadership, including analysis and commentary on group performance.
Assist with preparation of external reporting materials including results presentations, investor updates and other market communications.
Identify and implement improvements to financial planning tools, processes and reporting frameworks.
Skills & Experience
CA/CPA/CFA qualified.
Experience in FP&A, financial planning, corporate finance or a similar analytical role.
Strong financial modelling, analytical and problem-solving skills.
Advanced Excel capability and strong presentation skills in PowerPoint and Word.
Experience working with large data sets and business intelligence tools such as Power BI is desirable.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills with the ability to translate financial insights for senior audiences.

A new role means a new way to win together. We’ve been proudly serving Australian communities since 1914 and at the heart of our continued success lies our 115,000 team members working across the country. If you’re looking for a career-defining opportunity, take the next step and explore another way your work can have impact and make a difference.

About the team

People and Culture are dedicated to fostering  an engaged and empowered workplace. One that’s strong, diverse and where our team members have exciting career pathways to choose from. We do it by making people’s lives easier through better experiences, useful tools, and by providing ongoing opportunities to learn and grow.



Role Purpose

The Program Design Lead (PDL) owns and manages the end-to-end design, development, delivery, and continuous improvement of strategic, high impact learning programs. The role ensures learning solutions are aligned with the Coles Learning Strategy and Learning Framework, delivering cohesive, scalable programs that build capability across business units.
Working in partnership with senior stakeholders, subject matter experts, and external vendors, the Program Design Lead connects learning initiatives across disciplines to ensure consistency, impact, and alignment with organisational priorities.

This role requires strong project management discipline, the ability to influence stakeholders and accountability for vendor performance to ensure high-quality, on-time delivery of learning programs.
This is a key role in advancing Coles’ transition from fragmented learning offerings to a clear, cohesive learning ecosystem for all team members across brands. 


Key Responsibilities

Program Design and Development


Lead the design, development, delivery, and maintenance of strategic, high impact and BAU learning programs aligned to the Coles Learning Strategy and specific business requirements.




Ensure learning solutions are consistent with adult learning principles, the Coles Learning Framework and integrated across programs.




Act as the connective thread across learning initiatives and contribute to leadership development programs where required. 




Be a thought leader on the implementation and embedding of innovative design and development tools, technologies and solutions



Planning and Delivery


Develop and deliver against annual learning plans for the relevant business units or capability areas.




Coordinate with activity planning teams to ensure program schedules and realistic delivery timelines.




Work with central learning teams to advise scheduling, coordination, and rollout requirements of learning programs. 




Ensure programs are delivered on time and budget through solid project management practices.



Stakeholder Partnership


Partner with Capability Strategy teams, senior leaders, SMEs, program owners, and cross-discipline learning teams to design learning solutions aligned to business priorities.

Build and manage strong relationships with key stakeholders, including capability leaders, SME’s and business unit executives, to support development and delivery of learning initiatives.




Influence stakeholders, without formal authority, to drive adoption and implementation of learning solutions aligned to the organisational learning strategy.



Learning Evaluation and Continuous Improvement


Ensure program design quality and consistency across learning solutions.




Conduct post-implementation reviews of learning programs, producing clearly defined continuous improvement plans.




Use qualitative and quantitative insights and data analytics, to improve learning design, delivery, and embedding strategies.




Ensure embedding activities and behaviour change initiatives are implemented and measured. 




Ensure metrics are clearly defined, are built into program design and are measurable.




Establish reporting program requirements



Vendor and Project Management


Partner with external vendors to design and develop learning solutions that meet agreed quality expectations and align with Coles’ learning standards.




Manage learning program delivery through effective oversight of project plans, budgets, timelines, and key milestones.




Hold vendors accountable for meeting contractual commitments and ensure programs are delivered to agreed quality standards, timelines, and outcomes.



Self-leadership and Compliance


Demonstrate accountability for compliance with company values, policies, and procedures including the Coles Code of Conduct.




Complete required compliance training and contribute to a culture of learning excellence. 



Key Stakeholders


Capability Strategy & Partnering, Learning Design Innovation, and P&C teams 




General Managers and senior leaders in relevant business areas

Subject matter experts and program owners




External learning vendors and partners 




Experience and Qualifications



Minimum 5 years in Learning & Development, Organisational Development, Leadership Development, or HR.




Proven experience managing end to end design, development, and delivery of complex learning or capability programs.




Demonstrated ability to effectively manage multiple projects and priorities concurrently, while maintaining quality and deadlines.




Demonstrated ability to work at pace and with flexibility.




Experience managing budgets and vendor partnerships.




Ability to influence stakeholders across internal and external groups 




Qualifications



Certificate IV in Training and Assessment, or




Graduate or postgraduate qualifications in Human Resources, Adult Education, or Psychology (highly regarded), or




Equivalent workplace learning and development experience. 




What’s in it for you?

Flexible working options: We know that work is only one part of your life, so we actively encourage a positive work-life balance and provide hybrid working options to help you achieve it.
Office perks: Take advantage of our gym facility and fitness classes, free parking, BBQ area, mini-Coles supermarket, fooderie hub where you can sample new products before they hit the shelves, school holiday program and so much more when you come in.
Discounts: Eligible team members receive 5% discount all year round on your Supermarket and Liquor online and in-store purchases. We also offer additional periods of double discount (10%) at various times throughout the year. As well as hotdeals exclusive to team members that translate into additional savings.
Reward through recognition: Give and receive recognition, linked to our Coles values, through our digital recognition platform ‘mythanks’. You can accumulate points to redeem in the online shop for exciting gifts and electronic gift cards from an extensive range of retailers.
Opportunities for learning and development: No matter where you start within our diverse business, you’ll have experiences, exposure, and education to satisfy you. Discover and explore a variety of career development programs and job-specific training.
Paid parental leave: We understand how important your life outside work is and offer permanent team members paid parental leave to support you in balancing work and family.
Investment in your future: Our annual team member share plan offer allows eligible team members to make regular pre-tax salary sacrifice deductions to purchase Coles Group shares.

About the recruitment process

We’re continuing to build a gender equitable team, and a culture that is just as diverse, inclusive and welcoming as the communities we serve. We are committed to creating a workplace that is safe and respectful for our team. We encourage applications from people of all ages, cultures (including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples), abilities, sexual orientation and gender identities.

Your opportunity to join the Growth team in North Ryde

We are looking for a commercially minded senior analyst to join the Growth team in North Ryde, a role offering real exposure, variety, and the opportunity to help shape meaningful business decisions.This is a hands-on role where you’ll partner closely with stakeholders across the business, structuring ambiguous problems, building rigorous analytical models, and turning data into clear strategic and commercial recommendations that will influence the direction of the business. 

You will work with a range of stakeholders to tackle complex and consequential decisions. This isn’t a reporting role; you will use data to solve complex problems, build scenario models, and support commercial and strategic decision-making.  

You’ll bring depth across growth evaluation, financial and scenario modelling, and supply chain strategy — connecting operational realities to enterprise-level choices.

What you’ll do
Build rigorous financial, scenario, sensitivity, and decision-tree models to evaluate strategic options and surface key trade-offs for senior decision-makers
Provide analysis that contributes to pricing, margin improvement, and commercial initiatives, and support commercial agreements  
Partner with teams across strategy, operations, supply chain, and finance to solve business challenges and deliver commercial analysis and insight that supports strategy execution and confident decision-making
Analyse competitive, consumer, regulatory, and macroeconomic trends and translate them into clear strategic implications
Apply strong commercial thinking to connect the numbers to real business outcomes
Synthesise large volumes of internal and external data into concise, insight-led outputs for executive and leadership audiences
What you’ll bring
5–8 years in strategy, management consulting, or a senior analytical role within a complex commercial environment
A track record of structuring and solving complex problems — from defining the question to delivering the recommendation
Strong commercial acumen — an instinct for what drives value and the ability to connect analytical outputs to real business decisions
Advanced financial and analytical modelling capability, with strong data visualisation skills and comfort with large, complex datasets
An ability to leverage data, AI, and emerging tools to sharpen analysis and generate faster, deeper insight
Experience preparing executive-level communication — clear, concise, and persuasive
Proactive mindset, strong communication skills and ability to influence stakeholders
Background in food manufacturing, FMCG, supply chain, or agribusiness is advantageous, not essential
Why this role

You’ll have genuine influence on decisions that matter — working at pace, alongside senior leaders, on problems that don’t have easy answers. If you’re intellectually curious, commercially sharp, and energised by turning complexity into clarity, this is the environment for you.

We’re looking for people who are curious, caring, courageous, and committed, people who want to contribute their best work every day and help deliver food that’s… Always Good

Build data pipelines and deliver high-quality statistical insights.



Your new company
You'll join a large, stable organisation with a strong national footprint, known for investing in high-quality data, rigorous analysis, and evidence‑based decision-making. The culture values technical expertise, collaboration, and delivering trusted insights at scale.


Your new role
This role requires a specialist Data Scientist to support the ongoing development and use of a large-scale national data asset. The position is responsible for designing, building, and maintaining high-quality, robust administrative data pipelines that underpin outcome analysis across complex education and workforce pathways. It also requires advanced econometric expertise, including the application of causal and statistical methods to measure outcomes and support modelling activities.
The role is critical to delivering high-quality analytical outputs and to maintaining and leveraging a core data asset that underpins a wide range of analytical reports, system performance measures, and strategic insights at a national level.
The position applies advanced data engineering, data architecture, and confidentiality management skills to integrate and manage complex administrative datasets, ensuring accurate, reliable, and timely outputs for government and other stakeholders. By sustaining this infrastructure and applying advanced analytical capability, the role enables the consistent delivery of trusted, evidence‑based insights to support policy development and decision-making.



Key responsibilities:

Design, build and maintain high-quality, robust administrative data pipelines to support outcome analysis across complex domains.
Manage the ingestion, transformation and integration of large, complex administrative datasets from multiple sources, ensuring accuracy, consistency and reliability.
Apply advanced econometric, causal inference and statistical methods to measure outcomes and support evaluation and modelling activities.
Implement data quality controls, validation processes and documentation standards to ensure the integrity, confidentiality and reproducibility of analytical outputs.
Support the delivery of critical analytical outputs, including performance measures, reports and insights for government and other stakeholders.
What you'll need to succeed


Essential Criteria:

Demonstrated advanced coding skills across multiple programming languages.
Strong capability in database management, including working with large scale administrative datasets, data extraction, transformation and integration, and ensuring data quality, integrity and performance.
Proven experience designing, building and maintaining high quality, robust administrative data pipelines to support analysis across complex policy or service delivery domains.
Demonstrated experience applying advanced econometric and statistical methods, including causal inference techniques, to support evaluation and impact analysis in policy or service delivery settings.
Desirable criteria

Demonstrated experience working with vocational education and training (VET) and higher education data, supported by strong subject matter knowledge across both sectors and an understanding of relevant policy and administrative contexts.
Demonstrated practical experience working within the ABS Person Level Integrated Data Asset (PLIDA), including extracting detailed analytical outputs while adhering to ABS legislative, privacy and statistical disclosure control requirements.
What you'll get in return


Competitive Hourly rate

Flexible working arrangements

2x12 Month Extension Options available at department's discretion

This is a 12-month contract based in Sydney.

How you'll make an impact

Elicit, analyse, and refine business needs into clear user stories and data requirements
Define and document data requirements, business rules, and data mappings
Interpret and analyse data models, data flows, and datasets to support solution design

Manage and engage multiple stakeholders, ensuring clear communication, alignment, and expectation management

Focus Areas

Data discovery, analysis, and delivery across initiatives
Data modelling, mapping, and integration
Data validation, quality, and governance considerations
Supporting analytics, reporting, and data platform use cases
Enabling high-quality, trusted data for business insights

Your attributes

Proven experience as a Business Analyst in data-driven environments
Strong ability to translate complex data concepts into clear and actionable business requirements
Hands-on experience with data mapping, integration specifications, and data migration activities
Solid understanding of data structures, data flows, and business rules
Experience working across the data lifecycle, including data quality and governance principles
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills across business and technical teams
Ability to context switch effectively across discovery, delivery, and validation phases
Demonstrated collaboration with cross-functional data teams including engineers, analysts, and scientists

Familiarity with BI/analytics tools and data visualisation best practices
Experience in financial services, accounting, treasury, or risk domains

Job Title: Senior Manager - Market & Financial Risk Oversight and Assurance (Line 2)
Ad Text:
My client is looking for an experienced and strategic Senior Manager, Market & Financial Risk Oversight and Assurance to support the Head of Market Risk in leading the function. In this position, you will play a critical part in ensuring a robust and effective risk management framework across the Bank. You will bring deep expertise across liquidity, funding, interest rate risk, counterparty credit risk, capital management, and financial forecasting, providing independent challenge and insightful analysis. As a trusted subject matter expert, you will influence key forums including the Asset and Liability Committee and the Board, while championing a strong, positive risk culture.
  
About the Position
The Market Risk team is responsible for analysing, reviewing and overseeing market and financial risks across the Bank. Your key responsibilities in this position include:
  
Risk Oversight & Assurance

Overseeing Treasury and Capital risk activities including liquidity, funding, capital adequacy, IRRBB, and counterparty credit risk
Providing independent challenge on the effectiveness of risk frameworks
Supporting oversight of risk models and regulatory compliance including APRA standards
Leading reviews of risk self-assessments to ensure strong control environments
  
Risk Analytics, Reporting & Insights

Delivering high-quality risk reporting to regulators, Board, committees, and executive leadership
Analysing trends, concentrations, and emerging risks that impact the Bank’s risk profile
Ensuring risk events are accurately recorded, monitored, escalated, and reported
Leading targeted initiatives to enhance risk reporting and insights
  
Stakeholder Engagement

Partnering with business stakeholders to embed risk considerations into strategic decisions
Influencing outcomes through strong collaboration and clear communication
  
Continuous Improvement

Driving innovation and continuous enhancement of risk practices
Promoting a culture of constructive challenge and accountability
Ensuring that business changes are effectively integrated into risk frameworks
  
About You
To be successful in this position you will bring:

10+ years of experience in market risk, treasury risk, or balance sheet risk within an Australian regulated financial institution
Strong analytical expertise across financial instruments and risk types
Deep knowledge of liquidity, capital, IRRBB and counterparty credit risk
Proven leadership experience in developing risk frameworks aligned to Board risk appetite
Strong understanding of financial services regulation and compliance
Excellent stakeholder management and influencing skills
Strong communication skills with the ability to translate complex risk concepts
Highly analytical and data-driven mindset with continuous improvement focus
Ability to manage multiple priorities, projects, and stakeholders in a fast paced environment
  
If you would like more information or for a confidential discussion, please call James Wilson on 0414516089 or by email james.wilson@troocoo.com

This is a full-time five-year contract with the possibility of extension.

THE WORKING ENVIRONMENT

The Corruption and Crime Commission (CCC) is a leading anti-corruption agency whose primary purpose is to strengthen the integrity of the public sector, reduce corruption and disrupt criminal activity for the benefit of our community.

The Assessment and Oversight directorate is a multi-functional team working collaboratively across the Commission and the Western Australian (WA) public sector through its assessment, oversight and research and engagement functions.  The directorate manages the assessment of serious misconduct allegations, oversees action taken by public sector agencies into alleged serious misconduct and conducts research and analysis to inform Commission priorities and assist the public sector to mitigate serious misconduct.

THE OPPORTUNITY

As Assistant Director – Research and Assessments, you will play a key leadership role in guiding both the Research and Engagement team and the Assessment teams. You will provide strategic direction, ensure the integrity and effectiveness of assessment processes, and lead the development of innovative approaches that expand our prevention and education function and enhance public sector capability to reduce corruption.  

You will work closely with senior stakeholders across the Commission and the broader WA public sector, contributing to informed decision‑making, high‑quality reporting, and the continuous improvement of assessment and research practices. This is a rare chance to influence systemic integrity outcomes across government and support the Commission’s role in reducing misconduct and strengthening public trust.

If you are a collaborative leader with strong judgement, a strategic mindset, and a passion for public sector integrity, this role offers the opportunity to make a meaningful impact across the sector.

The Commission requires applicants to be Australian citizens or be immediately eligible for citizenship, and appointment is conditional on satisfactory completion of a rigorous security clearance process.

The Commission is an equal opportunity employer and encourages people with disability, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and persons from culturally diverse backgrounds to apply.

To learn more about the Commission and what we have to offer, please visit our website or you can follow us on Linkedin.

DO YOU HAVE ANY QUESTIONS ABOUT THE POSITION?

Enquiries about the position should be directed to Tracey Polmear, Director Assessments and Oversight on (08) 9215 4807.

HOW TO APPLY

Click on the “Apply for Job” button and attach the following:

A current and comprehensive CV which includes the names and contact details (phone number and email address) of two recent, work-related referees (including your current line manager)
A two-page statement addressing the role specific capability requirements from the Job Description Form (JDF) as listed below:
Significant experience in partnering with senior management to drive strategic priorities.
Experience leading teams to analyse complex problems and presentation of appropriate recommendations, based on consistent decision making.
Significant experience in managing prioritisation and continual development, specifically in the areas of research practices and reporting.

Applications must be submitted online – hard copy, late or proforma applications will not be accepted.   

If you are having any problems with the system, you may call Human Resources on 9215 4888.

Responsibilities
Unique opportunity with Aon’s Reinsurance team to support the comprehensive approach to risk assessment, risk transfer and risk mitigation
Effective catastrophe management is an essential component of an insurer’s risk management program.
Open to applicants Australia wide, Aon offers flexible working

Aon is in the business of better decisions

At Aon, we shape decisions for the better to protect and enrich the lives of people around the world.

As an organization, we are united through trust as one inclusive team and we are passionate about helping our colleagues and clients succeed.

 

What the day will look like

You will translate complex model outputs into clear, actionable insights to support client results, strategy, and capital decisions.  Using deep expertise across natural perils models and actuarial techniques, our analysts don’t just run numbers – they push the boundaries of what the models can do. By creatively overcoming data gaps and model limitations, they deliver a powerful, end‑to‑end view of each client’s portfolio and its true risk profile.

Constantly connected to all leading modelling firms, the team is at the forefront of industry change as it embraces emerging technologies, advanced analytics, and automation to continuously enhance the speed, accuracy, and impact of their insights for clients.

Key responsibilities include -  

Translating complex technical findings into clear insights and recommendations for internal and external stakeholders
Developing effective working relationships with internal and external clients; including managing client projects with overseas catastrophe modelling teams
Working with modelled outputs to determine suitable reinsurance strategies to mitigate all sources of loss (catastrophe, risk and attritional).
Refining catastrophe models and outputs by analysing clients’ historical data and latest scientific research.
Actively contributing to advanced analytics, climate and natural perils advisory projects.

How this opportunity is different

Work at the intersection of the science, risk and business with a direct impact on the management of natural hazards for our clients in Australia and New Zealand.

Together with our actuaries and brokers, our catastrophe team helps clients navigate the challenges of natural peril risk. This includes support for client reinsurance placements and optimising risk transfer strategies, plus on-going research into climate trends and catastrophe model adjustments.

 

 Skills and experience that will lead to success

Comfortable in client-facing, collaborative, consulting environment with excellent communication skills (both oral and written)
The ability to work well within a team, a curious mindset and strong problem-solving and analytical skills
Experience interpreting catastrophe modelling outputs and deliverables (preferred)
Insurance or reinsurance experience
Excellent organisation skills and ability to work under pressure
Self-discipline to manage own workflow and deadlines 
Advanced Excel skills and strong a working knowledge of MS Word, SQL and PowerPoint
Experience with Python, R, Tableau or AI tools such as databricks, an advantage but not essential
Tertiary qualifications in relevant field of study – natural sciences, engineering, actuarial science, mathematics or statistics

How we support our colleagues

In addition to our comprehensive benefits package, we encourage an inclusive workforce. Plus, our agile environment allows you to manage your wellbeing and work/life balance, ensuring you can be your best self at Aon. Furthermore, all colleagues enjoy two “Global Wellbeing Days” each year, encouraging you to take time to focus on yourself.  We offer a variety of working style solutions for our colleagues as well. 

 

Our continuous learning culture inspires and equips you to learn, share and grow, helping you achieve your fullest potential. As a result, at Aon, you are more connected, more relevant, and more valued.

 

Aon values an innovative and inclusive workplace where all colleagues feel empowered to be their authentic selves. Aon is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace.

 

Aon provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran, marital, domestic partner status, or other legally protected status. 

 

We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities and fostering an inclusive workplace. If you require accommodations during the application or interview process, please let us know. You can request accommodations by emailing us at ReasonableAccommodations@Aon.com or your recruiter. We will work with you to meet your needs and ensure a fair and equitable experience.

Take hold of a monster opportunity and join our Investment Operations and Performance team as a Senior Specialist - Valuations. If you’re a good fit for this role and live in Brisbane or Sydney, we’d love you to apply. 

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. As a Senior Specialist - Valuations, you’ll play an important role in managing valuation risk across the fund and ensuring the accurate and timely reporting of unlisted asset valuations to committees that oversee valuation governance. You will partner closely with investment teams, external managers and independent valuers to ensure strong, consistent valuation processes and high‑quality governance outcomes.




Day to day, you'll:

Oversee the valuation process across all investment asset classes, ensuring compliance with best practice and regulatory standards.
Review and challenge valuations from independent valuers and external managers, ensuring accuracy, strong judgement, and clear supporting documentation.
Contribute to the management of the composition of the Fund’s panel of valuers including coordinating RFPs, overseeing the RFP process of external managers for new assets or when rotation is required, all the way through to the engagement of an independent valuer.
Monitor markets and industries to identify when out‑of‑cycle valuations may be required.
Assess the impact of macro‑economic factors (e.g. interest rates, inflation, growth, and geopolitical risks) on asset valuations.
Support the Valuation Committee and contribute to Board and Committee papers.
Conduct valuation governance reviews for new and existing external managers to drive continuous improvement.

This role is available as a permanent opportunity.

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

Bachelor’s degree in finance, economics, accounting, or a related field.
Strong analytical and quantitative skills.
Proficiency in excel based analysis, financial modelling and valuation techniques.
Experience in managing multiple projects with a number of stakeholders and service providers.
Ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams.
Knowledge of relevant accounting standards.

About us

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Our inclusive culture means you’ll be valued and heard in a respectful workplace. We've created an environment where you can be yourself and do amazing work. It's that simple.

MUFG Pension & Market Services is a global business connecting millions of people with their assets – safely, securely, and responsibly. We have over 6500 employees across 4 continents and continue to grow every day.

MUFG Retirement Solutions is the largest business under MUFG Pension and Market Services and we are the leading administrator for super funds in Australia. Many of our clients are household names. We are well positioned within a growing superannuation industry, combining our rich legacy with a fast-paced environment that enables rapid career growth.

We are a diverse team with employees from various cultural and linguistic backgrounds, and we make sure to celebrate the successes of our clients and our people.

 

The Role


We’re looking for a highly skilled Senior Business Analyst to join our Portfolio Management team within MUFG Retirement Solutions. In this role, you will work closely with Project Managers, business leaders, cross-functional teams, and senior stakeholders to ensure business and technical requirements are captured, documented, and delivered across the full project lifecycle.

You will play a key part in shaping analysis practices, supporting continuous improvement across the Portfolio and Squad teams, and contributing to a culture of collaboration, quality, and delivery excellence. Your ability to navigate ambiguity, lead workshops, influence stakeholders, and produce high-quality documentation will be essential to your success.


Key Accountabilities and main responsibilities

 

Lead the definition of business problems by conducting in‑depth analysis across technical and non‑technical inputs. 
Develop clear, detailed business and technical requirements ensuring solutions meet objectives and quality standards. 
Maintain full traceability from requirements through development and testing. 
Facilitate workshops and discussions to gather, validate, and prioritise requirements. 
Analyse data to validate opportunities, inform decision‑making, and support business cases. 
Model business processes and identify improvements to enhance efficiency and customer experience. 
Assess impacted systems to determine required changes and guide developers on key considerations for accurate build. 
Produce high‑quality documentation including Business Requirements, Functional Specifications, and screen flows. 
Manage and maintain requirement artefacts to build organisational knowledge and support future project work. 
Analyse backlogs to identify synergies and improve efficiency across projects and workstreams. 
Contribute to and facilitate BA Centre of Excellence discussions and uplift initiatives. 
Support and/or lead functional testing activities, ensuring evidence, documentation, and strong traceability are maintained. 
Develop business‑level user acceptance criteria and support business readiness activities to ensure smooth BAU transition. 
Collaborate closely with technical and business stakeholders to review priorities and ensure alignment. 
Provide coaching, guidance, and leadership to team members, modelling strong communication and collaboration. 
Identify, assess, and manage project risks and issues, and champion strong RAID management practices. 
Provide recommendations for portfolio‑level improvements, efficiency gains, and enhanced delivery outcomes.




 

Experience & Personal Attributes

 

5+ years Technical Business Analyst experience, covering both business and technical requirements. 
Strong grounding in project management fundamentals (estimation, planning, resource management, risk & issue management, communications). 
Experience with project management and scheduling tools. 
Proficiency creating reports, charts, and presentations for executive stakeholders. 
Experienced user of Microsoft Office, ServiceNow, Jira, and SharePoint.
Strong understanding and experience with Agile methodologies. 
Ability to work confidently across multiple business and technical teams. 
Strong communication skills, with experience presenting to both large and small groups. 
Excellent analytical, problem‑solving, and documentation skills. 
Ability to manage competing priorities and adapt to change. 
Testing experience will be highly advantageous. 
Highly self‑driven, collaborative, and able to navigate

.




 

Some of our Employment Benefits

FlexiWorks – enabling our employees to work in the office and at home
Purchased Leave, Parental Leave, Volunteer Leave, Gender transition leave, Well-being leave
Employee Recognition Program - to recognise people who are demonstrating our values and Company purpose.

We are an inclusive employer whose people work collaboratively. We encourage, support and value the various talents and perspectives of our people and promote a flexible and blended work environment where our people can thrive, and their wellbeing is supported. We know that diversity drives better client outcomes, continuous improvement, and growth. Be part of our company and together we will achieve our full potential.

We treat all individuals fairly and equitably and do not discriminate on the basis of diverse characteristics including, but not limited to gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, ethnicity, cultural background, physical abilities/disabilities, religious or political belief, marital or family status or carers responsibilities.


How to apply


Ready to take the next step in your career? Apply now with your resume and detailed cover letter highlighting your skills, experience, and why you would be a good fit for the role.

Candidates must have the relevant work rights to be considered for an opportunity at MUFG Pension & Market Services. Successful applicants will be required to complete background screening prior to commencement of employment.

Ad Text:
As one of Australia’s largest super funds, we’re focused on delivering exceptional, personalised experiences that genuinely improve financial outcomes for our members. We’re now seeking an experienced Manager – Marketing Technology and Journeys to lead our MarTech and AdTech ecosystem and play a critical role in shaping the future of member engagement.

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. Reporting into the Senior Manager - Marketing Technology and Analytics, you'll be responsible for overseeing ART’s end‑to‑end marketing and advertising technology stack, ensuring it is high‑performing, well‑integrated and aligned to business strategy.


You’ll define and deliver future‑state MarTech roadmaps, manage platforms such as the Adobe Experience Platform and the Google Marketing Platform, and lead the data and audience team to enable sophisticated audience management, cross‑channel journeys and privacy‑safe measurement.


This is a highly collaborative role, partnering closely with IT, Digital, Design and Innovation teams to deliver seamless member experiences while fostering a culture of innovation and agility across marketing.

 

Day to day, you'll:

Define and execute ART’s MarTech and AdTech roadmaps, aligned to marketing and business goals.
Oversee the assessment, implementation and ongoing management of MarTech and AdTech tools including CDPs, journey tools, analytics platforms, clean rooms and ad platforms.
Lead and develop the data and audience team, building robust audience strategies to drive targeting, personalisation and journey effectiveness.
Manage the Google Marketing Platform (GMP) stack and publisher integrations to enable privacy‑safe tracking and measurement.
Ensure strong data governance, data quality and privacy compliance, partnering with IT on data incident management.
Identify and assess emerging technologies and AI, championing innovation within the MarTech ecosystem.
Act as a change agent, embedding agile ways of working and continuous improvement.
Fulfil risk management and Members Best Financial Interests (MBFI) responsibilities in line with enterprise guidelines.

This role is a permanent opportunity. 

📍 Location: Brisbane, Sydney or Melbourne. 

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

You bring extensive experience working in marketing technology and content‑led environments.
You’re confident managing complex MarTech and AdTech ecosystems at scale.
You’ve successfully developed and delivered MarTech roadmaps that align to broader business and marketing strategies.
You’ll have hands‑on experience with CDPs, journey orchestration tools, analytics platforms, clean rooms and ad platforms.
You bring strong expertise across the Adobe Experience Platform, Google Marketing Platform, including GA4, DV360, Campaign Manager 360 and SA360.
You have a deep understanding of audience management, data enablement and personalisation, and how these drive effective member journeys.
You’re experienced partnering with IT and digital teams to deliver seamless, integrated technology solutions.
You understand the importance of data governance, privacy and compliance, and you actively manage data quality and risk.

About us

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Our inclusive culture means you’ll be valued and heard in a respectful workplace. We've created an environment where you can be yourself and do amazing work. It's that simple.

Apply online now

Everyone is welcome to apply. We value diverse thinking, cultures, perspectives, backgrounds, and abilities. Awaken your career at Australian Retirement Trust and apply now. Applications close COB Wednesday, 22 April 2026. 

We don’t want to leave you with a monstrous wait. So, you’ll hear from us after the closing date to find out whether you’ve made it through to the next round or not. Being fair and up-front is important to us – and recruitment is no different.
