About The Role

Reporting into the Head of Technology within the Generosity Value Stream, you’ll be responsible for leading the delivery execution of model experiments and Gen Platform outcomes aligned to Model Driven Generosity (MDG). The MDG initiatives focus on enhancing customer experiences through personalised offers and innovative strategies.

You’ll lead a cross‑functional delivery team, enable strong agile ways of working, and partner closely with product, data, and technology stakeholders to deliver value at pace while balancing platform health and long‑term sustainability.

This role is a 12-month Fixed-term opportunity.

Responsibilities Include:

Lead and own delivery outcomes for a cross‑functional team, ensuring alignment to MDG objectives and project goals.
Enable and evolve agile ways of working, facilitating ceremonies and empowering teams to self‑manage.
Manage backlogs, delivery plans, risks, dependencies, and release coordination.
Provide clear delivery insights and progress updates to stakeholders across Technology, Product, and Data.
Remove impediments and support teams to make effective delivery commitments.
Partner with other Engineering Managers and leaders to drive cross‑team collaboration and continuous improvement.

About You

While we are big on development and offer on the job training, we want to set you up for success.

You’re a delivery‑focused engineering leader who thrives in complex, fast‑moving environments. You’re comfortable working with ambiguity, confident leading cross‑functional teams, and passionate about agile, value‑driven delivery. You build strong relationships and bring a strong sense of ownership and accountability to everything you do.

What You Will Bring

Proven experience leading delivery or engineering teams in agile, high‑volume environments.
Strong delivery leadership and management skills, with the ability to manage multiple workstreams and stakeholder expectations, driving people to the right outcomes.
An agile mindset, with hands‑on experience facilitating ceremonies and championing agile practices.
Ability to communicate complex technical and delivery concepts clearly to diverse stakeholders.
Understanding of engineering excellence principles and balancing delivery with platform sustainability.

The Perks

We work hard and play hard, so along with a competitive salary and generous performance-based bonus for permanent staff members, here’s some of our other perks:

25 days annual leave (that’s right, an additional week’s leave!)
Hybrid working policy (Monday and Friday from home) and a $1,050 home office allowance for new starters.
Industry leading Parental Leave support program with 26 weeks paid leave for Primary Carers and no wait time. We also have a progressive return to work benefit for your first 6 months back: come back for 3 days, get paid for 4; come back for 4 days, get paid for 5.
Hack Days - where your creativity and collaborative ideas lead to exciting new ideas and projects for our customers.
Access to outstanding career development programs, including expert-led internal training and external learning opportunities (staff allowance for personal development)
Weekly concierge service including free massages and nail appointments – all onsite in our Melbourne office!
Free breakfast, daily fresh fruit and weekly rotating socials, from Thursday office drinks to fun team events
End of trip facilities with onsite table tennis and pool tables
Discounted health insurance and free flu jabs yearly.

We review applications within the first two weeks, so apply now! Check out our Instagram and TikTok (@lifeatsportsbet), LinkedIn, and careers website for more information about our inclusive culture. 

Our Focus on Diversity, Equity & Inclusion

We don’t just support diversity – we celebrate it! This is a workplace where you can thrive and feel safe to bring your whole authentic self to work.  

What’s the Green Tick Feeling?  

Well, it’s different for everyone, yet unites us all. It’s the feeling you get when you succeed, no matter what success looks like to you. It's all the little and big things that make you feel like you’re thriving when working at Sportsbet.   
   
We’re an inclusive employer who welcomes you for who you are, as you are – so, if you require adjustments to the recruitment process, please let us know in your application. We also know flex means different things to different people, so let us know how we can support you to be your best.   

If you think you’d be a great fit but don’t necessarily meet everything in the job description, please still get in touch.

We support growing families with inclusive, carer-neutral paid Parental Leave of up to 16 weeks.

Direct your own development and build future-ready skills with Optus U's industry-focused micro-credentials, developed with La Trobe and Macquarie Universities.

Build meaningful connections through colleague-led networks and diversity initiatives including Culture Connect, Elevate Women, Disability Network, and Express Yourself (LGBTQIA+).

About the Agency
As the steward for digitally enabling Australia’s health system, the Agency plays a key role in coordinating national engagement, delivery, and adoption of digital health to facilitate person-centred, connected healthcare. Our responsibilities span national digital health services and systems, including My Health Record, Electronic Prescriptions, and the 1800Medicare App.

About the Role 
The Research, Analytics and Data (RAD) Branch is seeking a Director, Data Strategy and Governance (EL2) for a full-time ongoing position. Reporting to the Chief Data and Analytics Officer (CDAO), this role supports the CDAO and leads a geographically dispersed team responsible for developing and managing the Agency’s data policy, data strategy and data governance framework, aligned to the Agency’s work program. The role also oversees the delivery of the Agency’s scope of the My Health Record Research and Public Health Program.

Working closely with business areas across the Agency, the Director provides leadership, subject matter expertise and strategic support in relation to data strategy and governance. The role is responsible for defining and implementing data maturity uplift initiatives, including agency-wide data literacy programs, to address strategic data-related risks across the data asset lifecycle. The position supports branches across the Agency to ensure data is trusted, secure and safely accessible, enabling high-quality, informed decision-making. 


Skills and Experience

Demonstrated leadership in reform and change programs, including the design, implementation and embedding of policy, strategy and governance frameworks within multi stakeholder, government environments.
Extensive experience developing and delivering enterprise-wide data strategies, including leading data maturity and data literacy uplift initiatives and driving organisational change.
Demonstrated experience leading teams in a government setting, including geographically dispersed teams, with a focus on capability building and performance.
Strong knowledge of best practice in data strategy, policy and governance, including managing data-related risk to ensure data is trusted, secure and appropriately accessible.
Sound experience in program and project delivery, with a strong understanding of contemporary project management frameworks and methodologies.
Highly developed verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to provide advice, solve complex problems and effectively engage senior leaders and stakeholders.

For all queries relating to the responsibilities of the position, please contact Mike Lau at Mike.Lau@digitalhealth.gov.au.

Our Benefits
We offer a competitive 15.4% superannuation contribution, salary packaging options, and a Christmas closedown with no leave deduction. Our wellbeing program and EAP support a healthy work–life balance, while flexible work arrangements opportunities, including work from home, give you choice in how, when and where you work. You will enjoy modern collaborative workspaces and generous study support to employee who are eligible, as per our study assistance guidelines in our Enterprise Agreement. We also provide a wide range of leave options including parental, cultural, compassionate, emergency response and defence leave. EL staff may access time off in lieu (TOIL) for reasonable additional hours worked, subject to the recording of those hours.

Eligibility
Australian citizenship is mandatory. Candidates must be able to obtain and maintain a national police clearance and the required Negative Vetting 1 security clearance, with the ability to attain a higher-level clearance if needed.

Notes: Order of Merit: Applicants rated as suitable will be placed in a merit pool that may be used to fill similar ongoing or non-ongoing positions throughout the Agency for up to 18 months.

Application
To apply for the role, please submit your resume and a cover letter (1 page maximum) addressing the skills and experience requirements above and outlined in the position description. Please contact the recruitment team at careers@digitalhealth.gov.au with queries relating to the application process.

About the role

Brisbane Location

Hybrid work environment

Microsoft Fabric experience required

We are on the lookout for a dynamic Lead Consultant who is a recognized expert in their field across the broader industry. You will play a key role in shaping and delivering Modern Data Platforms that power Advanced Analytics and AI outcomes. You will lead the end-to-end journey—architecting, designing, developing, testing and delivering innovative, scalable data solutions—while working on complex, high-impact initiatives for leading organizations.

Responsibilities and Accountabilities:

Overseeing the end-to-end delivery, design, and development of Microsoft Fabric-based modern data platform architecture and solutions.

Complete responsibility for technical input, designing and developing smaller solutions and undertaking complex or more significant development activities within a larger project.

Providing mentorship and guidance to more junior AI & Data team members

Assist with building up the Fujitsu AI & Data practice, capabilities and offerings

Assist with pre-sales scoping, shaping of opportunities as well as developing proposals and presenting to clients.

Alert to opportunities for follow-on business and communicates identified new business opportunities for Fujitsu.

Study and remain up to date with relevant technological change and obtain certifications in skills used within the Fujitsu AI & Data team as agreed with your mentor.

Requirements:

Essential

Deep expertise in one or more of:

IoT

Data ingestion

Stream analytics

Microsoft Fabric/Data warehousing

Python

PowerBI / Power Apps

Agile/ Scrum

Good understanding of Data warehousing/Modern Data Platforms

Agile delivery methodology experience

Consulting skills

ETL development expertise

Outstanding technical skills ensuring you deliver value out of the underlying technology

Data Modelling expertise

Ability to manage and lead internal and/or external teams

Manage iterations, scope, priorities, stakeholders, risks, issues and timelines

Forming trusted advisor relationships with key stakeholders and customers

Why Fujitsu?

We are an organization with a strong set of values and a history of respecting fairness and equality, whilst promoting diversity, equity and inclusion. We call this Be Completely You.

Our customers trust us. We have an excellent reputation across the region and globally.

Best in class reward and recognition programs flexible work, volunteering leave and more.

Commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

As an inclusive employer, Fujitsu aims to recruit a diverse range of talents to help us achieve our purpose. In line with our diversity, equity, and inclusion strategy, we highly welcome applications from women and gender-diverse people; Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people; Māori and Pacific people; LGBTI+ people; people with a disability; culturally and linguistically diverse people; veterans, Australian Defence Force (ADF) and emergency responders. Transgender and gender-diverse applicants can request a copy of our Frequently Asked Questions﻿ to assist with the recruitment journey.

Senior Business Analyst – Insurance Platform Delivery
Location: Sydney CBD (Hybrid, 2–3 days in office)
Salary: Up to $150k base + super

Work on complex insurance platform implementations in a hands-on, client-facing environment.

About the Role
We’re hiring a Senior Business Analyst to support the delivery of insurance platform solutions across multiple clients. You will lead requirements gathering, run workshops, and work closely with product and engineering teams to deliver outcomes across policy, claims, and underwriting.

This is a hands-on role with real ownership, suited to someone comfortable working across the full delivery lifecycle in a consulting-style environment.

Key Responsibilities

Lead requirements gathering across policy administration, claims, and underwriting
Facilitate stakeholder workshops and drive clear outcomes
Translate business needs into functional specifications and user stories
Support solution design, testing, and UAT
Work closely with delivery, product, and engineering teams
Contribute to delivery standards and continuous improvement
Key Requirements

6+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst
Experience in general or commercial insurance
Strong exposure to policy, claims, or underwriting
Experience working in SaaS or platform delivery environments
Client-facing or consulting experience
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills
Experience with Agile delivery, user stories, and backlog management
Tools such as Jira, Confluence, and process mapping tools
Nice to Have

Experience with insurance platforms or policy administration systems
Exposure to integrations, reporting, or automation
Relevant BA or Agile certifications
Why Apply

Work on real platform implementations with direct client exposure
Strong mix of delivery ownership and stakeholder engagement
Modern, growing environment with a clear focus on quality delivery
Hybrid working model in Sydney CBD

THE COMPANY:

This opportunity sits within a large, privately owned Australian organisation with a strong market position, an international footprint, and a genuinely complex operating environment. With multiple business units, established core platforms, and an increasing focus on technology, data and digital capability, this is the kind of business where strong leadership can make a visible difference.

They are well regarded for the quality of their operation, the strength of their brand, and the scale of what they do. Importantly, they are at a point where the next phase of maturity across architecture, engineering and data needs the right leader in place — someone capable of setting direction, creating clarity and building capability for the long term. 


THE OPPORTUNITY:

Reporting to the CIO, this is a high-impact leadership role with enterprise-wide ownership across architecture, data and engineering.

This is not a narrow architecture position. Nor is it a purely strategic role removed from delivery. It is a broad, commercially acute mandate requiring someone who can define the target state, shape the roadmap, establish the standards and governance to support quality delivery, and build the internal capability needed to execute.

You will be central to how the business thinks about technology investment, platform evolution, data maturity and long-term sustainability. You will influence decisions across ERP, digital, e-commerce, integration and data platforms, while partnering with senior stakeholders across operations, finance, digital and risk.

For the right person, this is the type of role that offers real substance — the chance to step into a visible leadership seat, work closely with executive stakeholders, and leave a genuine mark on the future direction of the business. 


DAY TO DAY:

Lead the enterprise-wide architecture, data and engineering agenda

Define and maintain architecture principles, standards and guardrails across applications, integration, data, infrastructure and security

Own the target-state enterprise architecture and multi-year technology roadmap

Advise executive leadership on technology direction, priorities, investment and risk

Govern solution architecture across key initiatives, ensuring alignment with enterprise standards and long-term business objectives

Define and drive the organisation’s data strategy across platforms, analytics, reporting and integration

Establish a clear operating model for data ownership, stewardship, governance and accountability

Build and lead architecture, data engineering, analytics and BI capability across internal teams and partners

Translate operational and commercial priorities into scalable technology and data outcomes

Uplift engineering standards, delivery practices and quality controls across the software lifecycle

Oversee the ongoing evolution of ERP, digital, e-commerce, integration and data platforms

Manage budgets, strategic vendors and delivery partners

Recruit, mentor and lead senior architects, engineers and data specialists 


YOUR BACKGROUND:

This role is likely to suit an established Head of, GM or senior technology leader within the SME space (<500 employees) who has operated across enterprise architecture, data and engineering in a multi-system, business-critical environment.


You will bring:

Demonstrated leadership experience across architecture, data and software engineering

Experience working in complex medium to large organisations with multiple platforms, competing priorities and broad stakeholder groups

The ability to define strategy and translate it into clear, executable roadmaps

Deep understanding of data as an enterprise asset, including governance, quality, security and regulatory considerations

Strong experience building and leading high-performing multi-disciplinary teams

Commercial and financial capability across budget ownership, vendor management and investment prioritisation

The credibility and communication skills to influence senior stakeholders and articulate complex concepts in business terms

Experience across enterprise platforms such as ERP, digital, integration and data

Relevant tertiary qualifications, or equivalent practical leadership experience 


WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU?

A genuine enterprise leadership role with broad remit and executive visibility

The opportunity to shape architecture, data and engineering strategy in a meaningful way

A business with real scale, complexity and investment behind it

The chance to build capability, improve standards and influence long-term platform direction

Direct exposure to senior decision-makers and business-critical initiatives

A role with the breadth, profile and impact that rarely comes to market


HOW TO APPLY:


For a confidential discussion, please reach out directly or apply via SEEK.

This is an excellent opportunity for a senior technology leader looking for a role with real scope, strong visibility and the ability to influence both strategy and execution.

Super Retail Group is the proud owner of four iconic brands: Supercheap Auto, rebel, BCF and Macpac, and is one of Australia and New Zealand's largest retailers. The talent of our teams and their passion for providing an experience which inspires and enhances our customers’ leisure time drives our culture of innovation and opportunity. 

About the Role

At the centre of a high‑performing Supply Chain is the ability to turn data into action. As Performance & Insights Manager, you’ll shape how insight, analytics, and performance intelligence enable smarter, faster decision‑making across our supply chain operations.

This 14‑month max‑term contract, created as parental leave cover, offers a unique opportunity to make a significant impact in a strategic leadership role. You’ll define and execute the data and performance agenda—bringing together advanced analytics, business intelligence, and predictive modelling to unlock meaningful insights. By enabling leaders with clear, actionable intelligence, you’ll play a critical role in improving operational efficiency, strengthening financial outcomes, and supporting sustainable business growth through data‑driven innovation.

Your key responsibilities will include but not be limited to:

Developing and executing a comprehensive data and performance strategy that aligns with business objectives.

Developing and deploying data models, business intelligence tools, machine learning models and AI-driven solutions to optimize supply chain performance and provide meaningful insights.

Overseeing the enhancement of executive dashboards and real-time reporting tools in Power BI.

Collaborating with cross-functional teams to align data strategies with business priorities.

Leading and mentoring a team of analysts and data professionals, fostering a high-performance culture, as well driving capability development within the analytics function to keep pace with industry trends.

This role offers the flexibility to be based in Brisbane (Strathpine, preferred location), Sydney (Rhodes), or Melbourne with a hybrid work model.

About you

You’re a confident connector who thrives at the intersection of supply chain, commercial, and finance teams, enabling integrated and informed decision‑making. Comfortable engaging with a wide range of stakeholder groups, you communicate effectively across all channels—whether face‑to‑face or digitally. You bring a strong balance of technical capability and people leadership, allowing you to translate complex insights into action while guiding and supporting teams to deliver meaningful outcomes.

In addition, you have proven experience in the following:

A data science or pure maths commercial role, applying data science methods to business problems

Working with complex data sets and data relationships from multiple sources

Creating simple visualisations from complex data sets, matching visualisation style to data types and dimensions

Leading technical teams, ideally data engineers or scientists, in delivering a business service

Working with modern ETL and visualisation tools, preferably with experience in Alteryx and PowerBI, as well as SQL

A qualification in Data Science, Engineering or Mathematics

Highly desirable: Industry experience in Supply Chain, Warehousing or Logistics

WHY JOIN US? 

You’ll get to join for an organisation with a culture that is driven by innovation and creating awesome experiences for our customers and teams, and will get to work alongside a diverse team across Super Retail Group that is representative of the communities we work in. 

Super Retail Group is a Workplace Gender Equality Agency (WGEA) Employer of Choice for Gender Equality citation holder. 

We are committed to your professional development and will support on-going opportunities for both learning and progression. 

You will get access to generous discounts across all Super Retail Group brands, as well as access to our Perks Programme, including discounts on Health and Wellbeing, Entertainment, Travel and Accommodation and more. 

Our Culture of Inclusion

Super Retail Group is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We support, promote and celebrate diversity and we recognise the benefits a culture of inclusion brings to our workplace and customer experience

About the role – Full-time, Permanent

As a Program Test Manager- Payments, Cards and Fraud, join a high-impact transformation role where you will shape enterprise-wide test strategy and elevate QA maturity within a technology-enabled bank. Lead end-to-end testing across complex programs, driving automation, governance, and continuous improvement at scale. Partner with senior stakeholders and build a high-performing QA team to deliver quality outcomes that enhance customer experience.

Key responsibilities include:  

·        Leading enterprise-wide testing for a major transformation program: own the end-to-end test strategy and execution across multiple projects, shaping how quality is delivered in a technology-enabled bank.

·        Defining and embedding QA governance at scale by establishing testing standards, quality gates, and metrics that drive consistency, compliance, and measurable improvements in delivery quality.

·        Driving modernisation of testing practices – champion test automation, CI/CD integration, and continuous improvement initiatives to uplift QA maturity and accelerate delivery cycles.

·        Influencing program success through strategic leadership – partner with senior stakeholders to provide transparent reporting, risk management, and quality insights that directly impact go-live confidence and business outcomes.

·        Building and leading a high-performing QA team - mentoring Test Leads and Analysts, foster a collaborative culture, and create a motivated team aligned to delivering high-quality outcomes.

·        Orchestrate complex delivery across teams and vendors – coordinating cross-functional streams and external partners, ensuring seamless test execution, strong governance, and alignment to program objectives.

About you 

You will succeed in this role if you have:

·        Strong experience in payments, cards, and fraud testing including end-to-end transaction flows, system integration, and user acceptance testing, with a focus on risk, compliance, and production defect prevention. You will bring 4–5 years as a Test Manager leading senior test analysts, with proven success across large, complex programs.

·        Strategic and hands-on in equal measure: able to define test strategy and governance frameworks, while also understanding the detail of execution, tools, and methodologies.

·        Experienced in test automation frameworks and CI/CD integration, with a passion for driving efficiency, coverage, and continuous improvement.

·        People-first leader- a proven ability to build, mentor, and inspire high-performing QA teams, fostering collaboration, accountability, and continuous development.

·        Proactive in identifying risks, managing defects, and enforcing quality gates, ensuring strong governance and minimal production leakage.

·        Confident partnering with senior leadership, business owners, and vendors, translating technical insights into clear, business-focused outcomes.

·        Comfortable using metrics, reporting, and insights to drive decisions, improve testing maturity, and build stakeholder confidence.

Location

Whilst our primary office locations are in Adelaide and Brisbane, we are pleased to be able to consider applications from employees within Australia for this role. We also encourage you to put thought into how the work may be undertaken given your location and what support you may require.

Why you’ll love working with us: 

People First Bank – our new brand – says exactly what we’re all about: people. We're committed to supporting and growing our team and creating an outstanding work environment.

You’ll have access to financial and lifestyle benefits to support your success and individuality:

Flexibility and hybrid working arrangements.

Enhance your salary with discounts on banking, health insurance, and shopping across Australia.

Advance your career with training, study support, and project opportunities.

Competitive leave benefits that empower employees to take time for themselves and loved ones.

Wellbeing support with our Employee Assistance Program, wellbeing leave, mental health app, and mental health ambassadors’ program.

Make a difference with paid volunteering, Workplace Giving, and diverse community initiatives.

Be recognised for your contributions through our peer-driven recognition program.

 

A bit about People First Bank:  

In March 2023, People’s Choice and Heritage Bank merged to form Heritage and People’s Choice Limited - Australia's leading mutual bank.  We are now on an exciting journey to bring together our products, services, and technology under a single brand - People First Bank.

We have over 700,000 members and 1,900 employees, with dual head offices in Adelaide and Toowoomba, an Australian-based contract centre and branches across five states. 

With a vision ‘To deliver positive change through banking’, People First Bank serves members and contributes to the community, while offering an inclusive, flexible work environment. We’re big enough to make an impact; but agile enough to influence and do interesting work.

Company description:



Insignia Financial Limited is an Australian financial services company which offers a range of products and services including financial advice, superannuation, investment management and trustee services



Job description:



Lead one of Australia's fastest-growing wealth platforms at a key moment of scale, investment and transformation.

This is a rare opportunity for an experienced technology executive to lead a modern, successful platform with clear ambition, strong momentum and meaningful investment behind it.

Our Wrap platform (Expand) has established itself is a scalable, stable and high-performing foundation for growth, built on our proprietary technology and enabled by experienced delivery teams. The platform strength has been reflected in Wrap's rapid rise to 4th in the market and its clear ambition to become the market leader.

This role offers an experienced senior leader the opportunity to build on that foundation, sharpen the platform's commercial edge, scale capability, lift productivity and set a clear strategic view on where to invest next to support continued growth.

The opportunity to join the team
As General Manager Technology for the Wrap business, you will have end-to-end accountability for the technology function supporting one of Australia's largest and fastest growing wealth platforms managing over $100 billion in funds. Reporting to Liz McCarthy (CEO MLC Expand), you will operate as the business units senior technology leader, setting and executing a clear technology strategy aligned to Wrap's growth and commercial objectives.

You will lead the approximately 160 people across onshore and offshore teams. The group has a strong track record of delivery; the next phase is to lift engagement, build momentum, and evolve the operating model to support faster growth and higher productivity. You will also lead effectively across the organisation, partnering with senior leaders and teams across our business to deliver and continuously improve innovative retirement solutions at scale.

A key focus of the role is enabling adviser efficiency and client confidence through technology. You will own the technology roadmap, prioritise investments that make it easier for advisers to serve clients, and use data, AI and automation to simplify processes, improve decision-making and strengthen the client experience.

With the right investment and a large change agenda, this is a rare opportunity to shape how technology directly supports advisers, clients and the future of retirement in Australia.

What You Bring to This Role
This is a senior GM-level role suited to an established technology leader. To be successful, you will bring:

Proven experience leading large, complex technology functions with full accountability for strategy, delivery and operations, underpinned by a strong personal background in technology
Strong technical credibility and a deep understanding of modern platforms and the broader technology landscape
A track record of setting clear direction, prioritising investment, and delivering complex change in regulated environments
Experience building and leading high-performing, hybrid teams across onshore and offshore models
Practical, credible experience leveraging data and AI to lift productivity and capability
Strong stakeholder leadership and influencing skills, with the ability to partner effectively at executive level
A leadership style grounded in listening, coaching and clarity, with the confidence to make decisions and drive performance

Industry experience in regulated environments such as financial services, wealth or superannuation is highly valued, particularly where pace, scale and commercial agility have been critical.

Find the better way

What gets us excited about working here? Helping people feel good about their money as one of Australia's leading financial wellbeing organisations. From those starting out to those retiring. As your team, we'll show up for you and together we will create financial wellbeing for every Australian. Because we know people who are confident about their financial future live better lives.

If this sounds like your kind of career, you sound like our kind of person. With us, you'll do work that builds your technical know-how and challenges our entire industry to move forward. Along the way, you'll be free to explore new ideas and technology, solve problems in a team, and independently to get great things done. Around here, we like to call it "putting our fingerprints on the future".

People-inspired tech

Deep expertise combined with creativity can take on the trickiest of problems. Join our team of brilliant minds and mentors as we explore our way to innovation and apply technology in more human ways than ever.

__

To Apply

Read more here about why you should join our team.

Applicants will be required to provide evidence of their eligibility to work in Australia, and at a minimum be required to undertake police and basic credit checks as a condition of employment.

Please note that applications from agencies will not be considered at this time.

We acknowledge and celebrate the richness that individual differences bring to our team. If you need assistance or an adjustment during the application process, please reach out and let us know.

Job Summary:

We are looking for an experienced SAP CRM Functional Consultant with strong Utilities domain expertise to design, configure, and deliver SAP CRM solutions aligned with business and industry best practices. The role involves close collaboration with business and IT stakeholders to translate business needs into scalable SAP CRM solutions, with a strong focus on Utilities sales, service, and customer interaction processes.

Key Responsibilities

Lead Fit‑Gap analysis and functional workshops with Business and IT stakeholders.

Translate business requirements into functional and solution designs aligned with SAP CRM Utilities best practices.

Provide functional leadership and advisory support on SAP CRM architecture and solution design.

Bridge functional and technical gaps by collaborating closely with ABAP and integration teams.

Identify, design, and implement SAP CRM solutions that meet business objectives and quality standards.

Produce and maintain high‑quality Functional Design Documents (FDDs) and Solution Design documentation.

Support system configuration, testing, defect resolution, and production issues.

Act as a key point of contact for functional decision‑making and stakeholder communication.

Required Skills & Qualifications

Strong functional expertise in SAP CRM within the Utilities domain.

Experience working with at least one SAP IS‑U module and CRM–IS‑U integrations.

Proven ability to conduct Fit‑Gap analysis and lead stakeholder workshops.

Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills.

Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, including interaction with senior business and IT leaders.

Ability to work effectively across cross‑functional teams and distributed delivery models.

Collaborative mindset with a strong focus on solution quality and business outcomes.

Benefits:

Joining Cognizant will give you the opportunity to learn and collaborate with some of the most talented people in the industry, while having your finger on the pulse of emerging industry trends and working on the cutting edge of technology in your field of expertise.

We recognize that our people perform at their best when they feel valued as significant contributors and that is why at Cognizant, taking care of our employees is a priority:

·   You can pursue innovative career tracks and opportunities here

·   You can enhance your professional development through education and dedicated training

·   We’ll give you the skills you need to keep pace with the changing workplace while our compensation, benefits and wellness packages help you stay healthy and plan for the future.

Next Steps:

Please reach out to our friendly and welcoming team today to apply and register your interest for this full-time role. We'd love to help you get your next role and enable you to fulfil your professional ambitions.

Job Summary
Join a forward-thinking team as an Senior Investment Analyst, where your expertise will shape governance standards across multiple RSEs. This role offers the opportunity to lead investment policy management, provide strategic oversight, and influence trustee decision-making. Ideal for an experienced professional looking to make a tangible impact within a respected financial services environment.

Benefits

Lead the development and refinement of the Trustee Investment Governance Framework (IGF), ensuring it remains aligned with evolving legislative standards and industry best practices.
Engage directly with senior stakeholders and investment managers to shape investment strategies and approval processes.
Take ownership of due diligence processes, preparing reports that influence trustee decisions across a diverse asset base.
Contribute to ongoing product enhancements and new offerings, supporting the trustee’s commitment to client outcomes.
Be part of a high-calibre team, operating within a structured environment that values governance, compliance, and precise reporting.
Work in a role with significant influence on investment policies, with clear pathways for professional growth and development.
Key Responsibilities

Establish and maintain the Trustee Investment Governance Framework (IGF) for all RSEs.
Monitor adherence to policies, frameworks, and legislative compliance across the investment lifecycle.
Assist in annual reviews of investment strategies, including stress testing under SPS 530.
Provide expert support and strategic advice on investment issues to trustee teams and clients.
Lead the investment approval process, ensuring consistent standards are applied to all decisions.
Liaise with asset consultants and investment managers to ensure compliance and high standards in reporting and oversight.
Requirements

Tertiary qualification in Finance, Commerce, Economics or a related discipline.
Minimum of 5 years’ experience in financial services, with a focus on investment monitoring and governance.
Proven experience establishing and maintaining trustee investment frameworks (IGF).
Strong report-writing skills and the ability to provide clear, actionable advice.
Demonstrable knowledge of APRA’s SPS 530 standards and legislative requirements.
Experience working directly with investment managers and fund consultants.

Working closely with executive sponsors, data and technology leaders, and business stakeholders, you will establish and embed enterprise‑wide data governance frameworks, standards and stewardship practices. Build data literacy and foster a culture where data is treated as a strategic asset

Significant experience leading data governance, data management or information management initiatives

A well-established and expanding property development group is seeking a Development Finance Analyst to join their high-performing finance team. This role will suit someone with strong feasibility modelling capability, a commercial mindset, and a genuine interest in property development.

You’ll play a key role supporting acquisitions, evaluating project feasibility and providing financial insights that help shape investment and development decisions across a diverse residential pipeline.

The Opportunity

Working closely with senior finance and development leaders, you’ll contribute across feasibility modelling, project reporting, due diligence and financial analysis. The role offers exposure across the full development lifecycle within a collaborative, entrepreneurial environment.

Key Responsibilities

 

Build and maintain detailed feasibility models (Estate Master DF/DM)
Conduct scenario analysis, cashflow forecasting and sensitivity testing
Support acquisitions, joint ventures and development financial modelling
Maintain project accounting data and ensure financial accuracy
Prepare board papers, drawdown documentation and reporting packs
Track project performance vs feasibility assumptions
Collaborate with development, finance and external stakeholders
Improve modelling templates, processes and reporting tools
About You
Property development, construction or real estate finance experience
Strong Estate Master capability essential (DF/DM)
Advanced Excel and financial modelling skills
Understanding of development feasibilities and investment analysis
Accounting background beneficial (CA/CPA desirable)
Strong commercial acumen and analytical thinking
Ability to work in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills

What’s On Offer

Exposure to a growing development pipeline
Collaborative, entrepreneurial team environment
Broad involvement across acquisitions and project delivery
Modern Melbourne head office location
Strong career growth potential

Performance and Reward Partner

Be part of a team that enables our business strategy through our evolving performance and reward approach

Flexible / Hybrid Work Arrangement!

Bella Vista location - Free parking onsite




We Are Woolworths Group 

We are Woolworths Group. 200,000+ bright minds, passionate hearts and unique perspectives connected by a shared Purpose – ‘to create better experiences together for a better tomorrow.’ It’s that Purpose that fuels our ambition to explore new ideas, make brave commitments and innovate better ways to meet the food and everyday needs of more than 24 million customers every week. 




If you’re excited to turn today’s blue sky thinking into a better tomorrow for future generations, you’ll find yourself supported and enriched in a dynamic, inclusive and empowering workplace that reflects the diverse communities we serve. With a culture of genuine care, a flexible approach to work and opportunities across the group to grow your career and make a meaningful impact, the possibilities for what we can achieve together are endless. 




What you’ll do:

As a Performance and Reward Partner, you will work closely with key stakeholders to enable our performance and reward strategies across your business group. You will act as a trusted advisor, designing and recommending performance and reward solutions that are fit for purpose and strategically aligned. You’ll play a pivotal role in driving business success by harmonising complex reward strategies and fostering a high-performance culture across a diverse, evolving portfolio.

Provide leadership and coaching to your direct report, empowering them and driving career growth

Work collaboratively with peers to ensure a high quality and consistent offering to stakeholders

Design and implement innovative reward strategies, providing sound advice and performing the role of trusted advisor in recommending high quality solutions

Maintain a keen focus on continuous improvement, anticipating and applying emerging trends, contemporary practices and prioritisation of the highest value work

Oversee the full annual reward lifecycle, including salary planning, benchmarking, and performance reviews, ensuring alignment with the broader Group framework

Lead and contribute to significant programs of work, projects and one-off solutions that provide meaningful insight

Provide high-level subject matter expertise and thought leadership to complex reward and market movement matters



What you’ll bring:
Demonstrated experience in a similar role with a deep technical understanding of job evaluation, salary structures, and contemporary reward philosophies.
The ability to translate complex business objectives into competitive reward strategies while maintaining strong commercial acumen.
Extensive knowledge of reward programs, including regulatory requirements 
A data-driven approach to your work, providing deep insight that ensures our reward offerings remain competitive and impactful.
Broad experience across industries, company size and complexity




What you’ll experience

A global business with endless career possibilities around every corner and across every discipline – with valuable exposure to a vast and exciting business network.

Access to Sonder.  Sonder provides free confidential 24/7 personalised financial, medical safety, psychological or physical support for team members and their families.

A progressive and competitive leave policy that gives you more space for what matters to you.  

Team discounts across our range of Woolworths Group brands you know and love and a robust rewards program that celebrates and incentivises purpose-driven work.

Opportunity to Salary Sacrifice, enabling team members to purchase selected eligible items or services from pre-tax salary.  Can be used for novated leases, superannuation contributions, Qantas Club membership, computing devices etc.




Everyone belongs at Woolworths Group

Diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging are key to realising our purpose of better together for a better tomorrow. We recognise the value our team’s diversity brings to our business, customers, and communities and that teams with diverse experiences and backgrounds enrich our group and are better able to innovate and solve problems. As one of the largest employers in Australia and New Zealand, we aim to create a truly inclusive workplace where everyone feels that they belong, can be their best selves, and reach their full potential.

Join Our Client as a Corporate Reporting & Insights Specialist

Are you a data-savvy professional with a talent for transforming complex information into clear, compelling insights? Our client is seeking an experienced Corporate Reporting & Insights Specialist to support executive, Board, and regulatory reporting within a large, complex, and regulated organisation. This critical role involves not only traditional corporate reporting responsibilities but also providing in-depth technology analytics related to assurance, governance, and systems data - contributing to strategic decision-making in a high-profile environment.

What You'll Do:

Coordinate and produce key divisional and executive reports, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and timely delivery.
Analyse both qualitative and quantitative data, including technology-related metrics such as system performance, assurance, and governance indicators, to support reporting and strategic insights.
Develop clear, concise narratives that communicate complex information effectively to senior leadership and stakeholders.
Support the development and update of strategic documents like corporate plans and strategy maps.
Engage with various stakeholders across technology, assurance, and operations teams to gather accurate data and insights.
Ensure all reporting complies with relevant standards, regulatory obligations, and organisational policies.

Required Skills:

Proven experience in corporate, executive, or committee reporting roles, with a strong ability to develop dashboards and visual data presentations.
Expertise in analysing data and translating it into clear, engaging narratives suitable for an executive audience.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex information.
Experience working within large, complex, or regulated environments.
Confidence in engaging with senior leaders and managing multiple stakeholders simultaneously.
Experience with technology performance metrics, assurance processes, governance data, or risk reporting is highly desirable.

Nice to Have Skills:

Exposure to safety-critical sectors such as infrastructure, aviation, utilities, or government agencies.
Experience working with technology governance, assurance, or systems engineering data.
Advanced skills in reporting tools, dashboards, or data visualisation platforms.

Other Requirements:

Flexible location with hybrid working arrangements in Canberra, Brisbane, or Melbourne.
Ability to travel when necessary.
Demonstrated commitment to safety leadership and adherence to organisational policies.

This is a unique opportunity to deliver high-visibility reporting and insights that influence organisational strategy. If you are a proactive, collaborative professional with a passion for data analysis, storytelling, and technological insights, we invite you to apply now and contribute to impactful work in a leading organisation.

Identify issues and lead client management interviews

Manage writing the business valuation report

Mentor, manage and support junior members of the team; provide effective guidance, answer queries, review work and support them in progressing their career

We put a lot of energy into developing meaningful relationships with our clients and each other. It results in a level of trust that creates surprising opportunities for our business. And your career. 

Bring your best ideas 

We're inspired by potential. We know there's always a better way to do something. So we foster an environment of openness. Great ideas can come from anywhere. It all starts with listening.

About the role 


Ventia’s Finance team is seeking a full‑time Senior SAP Risk & Security Analyst to support our SAP Security Assurance function. Based in Cremorne, VIC (or any major state), this role ensures the integrity, accuracy and risk governance of SAP data, access, and controls across Ventia’s SAP landscape.

What you’ll be doing


Lead data remediation activities across SAP ECC and BTP, ensuring accuracy, completeness and compliance.
Manage and analyse role, access and data integrity issues, identifying root causes and implementing corrective actions.
Deliver daily reporting on Segregation of Duties (SoD), Sensitive Transactions and associated mitigating controls.
Investigate and remediate SoD and Sensitive Access risks, improving data quality and role design processes.
Maintain and enhance Ventia’s GRC solution (Soterion), including ruleset updates and control optimisation.
Partner with IT, business stakeholders and external providers to resolve data and access issues efficiently.
Identify opportunities to uplift the risk, control and data governance environment, aligned to frameworks and best practice.

About you

You bring experience in data remediation, risk analysis or SAP security within a large or complex organisation, ideally in infrastructure or Defence‑related environments. You also have:

Strong understanding of SoD, Sensitive Transactions and data remediation practices
Advanced analytical capability to interpret data across multiple systems and translate insights into action
Experience delivering end‑to‑end remediation or risk projects within governance frameworks
Exposure to internal/external audit, risk management and compliance
Knowledge of IAM, SAP authorisations, ERP data structures and GRC processes
Ability to work flexibly within a small, high‑performing team

All Ventia Defence contract roles require Australian Citizenship and eligibility to obtain and maintain a Defence Security Clearance.

Diversity


Ventia is enriched by the diverse experiences, talents and cultures that our people bring to the organisation, while celebrating and respecting the communities we operate in. We are committed to providing a safe and positive environment that provides equal opportunities for individuals of all backgrounds, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, Māori and Pasifika people, LGTBQI+, Women, Veterans and spouses and people with disabilities.

What We Offer You


Not only do we offer a competitive salary and career growth, we have also partnered with some reputable organisations to be able to offer discounts on Health Insurance, Banking (new home loans, credit cards, everyday bank accounts), flights & accommodation, car hire, car leasing and much more.

Job Title: Chief of Staff to CFO - Business Analyst / Commercial Analyst
Ad Text:
Chief of Staff to CFO - Business Analyst / Commercial Analyst

Start your career where it actually makes an impact.

At 1KOMMA5°, we’re building the energy company of the future—helping households transition to clean, affordable energy powered by sun and wind.

We’re looking for a Business Analyst early in their career, who’s strong with numbers, commercially curious, and ready to step out of purely technical work into a role where your insights directly influence real business decisions.

If you’re currently in investment banking, Big 4, consulting, or a graduate commercial role, this is your chance to accelerate your career in a fast-moving, high-growth environment.

The Role:

As Chief of Staff, you'll work closely with the CFO to support analysis, reporting, and commercial decision-making across the business. The role will assist in ensuring strategic projects are appropriately analyzed, achievable and beneficial to the company from a financial persepective. It is ideal for someone who wants to learn fast, take ownership early, and gain exposure across multiple areas of a growing company.

What You’ll Be Doing:
Manage and implement strategic projects across Finance, Legal, Procurement, Sales, and Operations
Act as a key coordination point between the CFO, Finance team, internal stakeholders, and external partners
Prepare executive-level reports, presentations, and decision-making materials, and ensure follow-up on actions
Identify and implement process improvements to increase efficiency and scalability across business functions
Support financial, commercial, and operational analysis, including KPI tracking and ad-hoc reporting
Assist the CFO in prioritising and managing key workstreams to ensure focus on high-impact initiatives
About You:
1–3 years minimum experience in investment banking, Big 4, consulting, or a business analyst/commercial analyst role
Degree in Finance, Business, Economics, or similar
Strong Excel skills and confidence working with data
Solid analytical thinking with structured way of working
Clear communicator who can explain numbers in a simple way
Proactive attitude with a willingness to learn and take ownership
Organised and able to manage multiple tasks
Bonus Points:
Exposure to financial modelling or forecasting
Experience with PowerPoint, dashboards, or BI tools
Interest in renewable energy or sustainability
Why Join Us?

Be part of a globally recognised renewables company that is driving positive change and making a real impact on the future of energy.
North Sydney location, working very closely with the Australian CFO.
Collaborative and innovative company culture, where your ideas and expertise will be valued and recognised.
Reporting directly to the CFO, on offer here is a base salary of up to $120k + super, commesurate with experience.  

To be considered for this position send through an application today.

Job Title: Principal Planning Analyst, Strategy and Planning Team
Company: Cairns & Hinterland Hospital and Health Service
Category:
Strategy & Planning (Consulting & Strategy)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
About us:

The Strategy and Planning team provide strategic leadership, strategic direction and coordination for planning initiatives across Cairns and Hinterland Hospital and Health Service, to meet the current and future health care needs of our community.

Cairns Hospital is the largest major hospital in Far North Queensland, caring for patients from the Mossman/Port Douglas to Tully in the south and out to Croydon in the west. 

Our population is 260,000 with First Nations people making up 14% of the region's population. Cairns Hospital provides a range of specialist outpatient, inpatient, and emergency services from its CBD site on the Cairns Esplanade. The hospital has 506 inpatient beds and 53 emergency department treatment spaces, 16 ICU beds, 22 special care nursery beds, 48 inpatient mental health beds and 25 maternity beds.

Cairns offers a widely-varied lifestyle in a tropical environment boasting several local landmarks and is the perfect base from which to explore the tropical north. Popular activities include exploring the World Heritage-listed Great Barrier Reef and the Daintree rainforest, as well as the Atherton Tablelands to the west of town and the Port Douglas region to the north. Cairns is also a well-known venue for sporting events, such as mountain biking competitions and marathon festivals.

We're committed to improving the health and wellbeing of people in Far North Queensland by providing high quality acute health care services.

About your role:

Extract large data sets from multiple sources, analyse and interpret results to inform planning requirements for population health projections, local needs assessment, geographical or health infrastructure planning, specific population group planning, health demand and scenario impact projections.
Undertake research and environmental scanning activities to proactively support the delivery of innovative, enhanced/ alternative models of care, or service re-orientation in line with local, state, and national directives.
Manage and coordinate designated data analysis projects, undertaking responsibility for planning, scoping, and scheduling activities, and completing projects in accordance with agreed timeframes and deliverables.
Provide analysis and feedback on Federal and State policy and planning documents, such as guidelines, frameworks, benchmarks and ensure their correct application when producing planning artefacts.

For more information about this role, please refer to the attached Role Description.

Vaccine Preventable Diseases (VPD) evidence as required for your employment in accordance with legislation/government policy and Directives.

What we offer:

Cairns and Hinterland Hospital and Health service offer a number of workplace benefits. Besides attractive remuneration, generous superannuation, salary packaging and career development opportunities, we value a healthy work/life balance that allows you take advantage of the sunshine, hiking tracks, swimming spots, reef and rainforest. 

Learn what it's like to work with us, in an inclusive and supportive work environment.

Discover our dynamic and culturally diverse region through community and lifestyle.

Listen to what some of our staff have to say about working in our regions.

Visit our website to learn more about our hospitals and health centres.
